title: Orbital nightcloak

{{Quote|On its brightest day, Dathomir is a shadow world. But the infernal nightcloak filled our skies with black blood and swallowed the moon we worshiped.|[[Ute Raek]], [[Singing Mountain Clan]] [[Priest/Legends|priestess]]|<ref name="NEGWT" />}}
The '''orbital nightcloak''' was a siege weapon deployed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. It consisted of a network of hundreds of satellites orbiting a targeted [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. When placed in orbit around a world, the satellites would prevent all sunlight from reaching the surface, creating utter darkness and eventually turning the world to solid ice, killing all life.

==Description==
{{Quote|The Orbital Nightcloak intercepts visual light and other EM wavelengths, plunging a planet into a night that never ends... until you decide it can.|Bevel Lemelisk|<ref name="Imperial Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Orbital_Nightcloak_Schematics_EGWT.png|thumb|left|250px|The schematics for the orbital nightcloak]]
The orbital nightcloak was one of the many [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]] designed by the [[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]] for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="NEGWT">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> The IDMR took their [[visual electromagnetic intensifier]] design, made adjustments to it and relaunched it as the orbital nightcloak. Where the VEI was intended to focus visible light onto a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] and effectively burn the surface, the nightcloak was designed to perform the opposite function.<ref name="IH">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>

Intended as a siege weapon,<ref name="EGWT" /> the superweapon was an array of hundreds of individual [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] that were deployed into the [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of a target planet. Orbital nightcloak satellites were transported to a planet via [[Starship/Legends|starship]],<ref name="NEGWT" /> usually nothing smaller than a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]].<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> Once at a target world, the satellites were released and used [[Thruster/Legends|deployment thrusters]] to move into a precise predetermined position in low orbit as part of the larger network.<ref name="NEGWT" />

When active, each satellite deployed a large dish that was comprised of [[electromagnetic absorption panel]]s. These panels were capable of absorbing and distorting light waves, preventing any light energy reaching the surface of the planet, including visible, [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]], and [[Ultraviolet/Legends|ultraviolet]].<ref name="NEGWT" /> When off-line, these panels formed a dome over the satellite's [[Discharge Focusing Rod]] and [[Discharge Modulator]]. A long stem projected from the underside of the panels, containing the rest of the satellite's systems. A [[Discharge Adjuster]] sat at the top of the stem just underneath the absorption panels. A [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]] provided power to the deployment thrusters, and other systems included a [[Subspace transceiver/Legends|subspace transceiver]], a [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]], and a [[Telemetry Node]] at the very bottom of the stem. Each satellite was powered by [[Star/Legends|solar]] energy stolen from its target;<ref name="EGWT" /> once in place, the orbital nightcloak was completely self-sufficient and remained in perpetual operation.<ref name="NEGWT" />

Each nightcloak satellite was equipped with an array of [[Sensor jammer/Legends|sensor jammers]] designed to block all [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and methods of communications. Residents of the planet were unable to call for help, and they could not receive information from the outside in return. The nightcloak did not prevent physical passage, and starships could move freely through nightcloak's effects. The Empire deployed [[Warship/Legends|warships]] outside the cloak to intercept any vessels attempting to flee or assist the world.<ref name="NEGWT" />

[[File:OrbitalNightcloakSatellites-FF35.png|thumb|right|250px|A pair of Orbital Nightcloak satellites]]
From the surface of the planet, the inhabitants viewed the deployment of the nightcloak as areas of impenetrable darkness that steadily grew to blot out the sun.<ref name="EGWT" /> Without the warming light of its star, a planet shrouded by an orbital nightcloak was plunged into neverending darkness. The effectiveness of a nightcloak resulted in complete compliance from the inhabitants of the planet. On primitive worlds, the population almost immediately swore obedience to the Empire<ref name="NEGWT" /> as they had no understanding of the technology behind used against them.<ref name="EGWT" /> More civilized worlds held out longer, but eventually the climatic changes to the planet caused by the nightcloak became too much for them to bear.<ref name="NEGWT" /> Even knowing what was happening, the inhabitants of the world were unsettled and frightened.<ref name="EGWT" />

The impact on the planet's climate began immediately; surface temperatures began dropping immediately, with snow beginning to fall in areas that had recently been temperate.<ref name="EGWT" /> Four [[Standard day/Legends|days]] was long enough for the temperature to drop low enough to kill off the majority of life on the planet,<ref name="FF35">{{FactFile|35|ORB 1-2|Imperial Orbital Nightcloak}}</ref> and after as little as three [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]], the temperature of the entire planet was freezing.<ref name="EGWT" /> [[Plant/Legends|Plant]] life began to die off, eventually causing a collapse in the entire food chain.<ref name="NEGWT" />

Deployment of the orbital nightcloak into position took several [[Standard day/Legends|standard days]], involving the direction of hundreds of satellites. The main weakness of the orbital nightcloak was that it could be easily disabled by the destruction of just a few of the satellites, as the control systems were interdependent.<ref name="EGWT" /> The satellites themselves were not equipped with defensive measures, and relied on orbiting Imperial vessels to protect them.<ref name="NEGWT" /> [[Starfighter/Legends|Starfighters]] were capable of destroying the satellites, and they were also vulnerable to anti-orbital defenses. Imperial [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] also introduced "dummy" satellites into the network. These were decoy units that could be destroyed that would have no effect on the rest of the network.<ref name="ISB" />

The [[Imperial Remnant]] made improvements to the nightcloak design after the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The array was redesigned with an advanced routing system that allowed the nightcloak to remain operation with ninety percent of its satellites operational. The improved satellites, designated [[Mark II satellite]]s, were also armed with short-range [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computers]].<ref name="NEGWT" />

==History==
{{Quote|The planet Dathomir holds many Force users of great power. However, the Emperor found it before we did and he has blockaded the planet.|Urai Fen|<ref name="FoC">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''</ref>}}
[[File:OrbitalNightcloakDathomir-FF35.png|thumb|right|250px|Nightcloak satellites surround Dathomir]]
The orbital nightcloak was designed by the Imperial Department of Military Research to fulfil the goals of the [[Tarkin Doctrine/Legends|Tarkin Doctrine]]. The Doctrine outlined that the most effective weapons used against the enemy not only killed them, but subjected them to fear and hopelessness at the same time.<ref name="NEGWT" /> Due to the network's limitations, the orbital nightcloak was intended for deployment against unarmed and non-technologically advanced planets. Once a target planet had capitulated or all life had been killed off, the Empire would remove the nightcloak and then exploit the world's resources.<ref name="FF35" />

Orbital nightcloaks were deployed around the planet [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] as part of the Imperial [[blockade of Dathomir]].<ref name="FoC" />

[[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] used an orbital nightcloak in an unorthodox manner. Deploying a number of satellites to create a corridor of impenetrable darkness, Zsinj used the sensor deadzone to hide the escape of his [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class star Dreadnought]], ''[[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|Iron Fist]]'' during the [[Battle of Selaggis]]. Zsinj detonated a skeletal structure of wreckage from the ''[[Razor's Kiss]]'' to fool the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] into believing the ''Iron Fist'' had been destroyed.<ref name="SC">''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''</ref>

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the orbital nightcloaks were activated by Zsinj over Dathomir, holding the planet hostage until the inhabitants handed over [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Solo escaped Zsinj's clutches and succeeded in disrupting the chain of the nightcloak satellites, saving the planet from being frozen.<ref name="COPL" />

At some point, [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] took down an entire network of orbital nightcloak satellites utilizing one [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing]] firing a single [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]]. [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] made note of the accomplishment in a heavily annotated copy of the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' possessed by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] forces, underscoring a section that stated the nightcloak's vulnerabilities. Another comment, left by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], asserted that a starfighter assault was not even needed. A [[Commando/Legends|commando]] armed with a [[Homing missile/Legends|homing missile]] could lock on and destroy a satellite in orbit from the surface of the planet.<ref name="IH" />
